expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “1) Case focus…”, the output states the Phase 4b contract requires grouping into a canonical taxonomy while retaining scenario granularity, quoting: “Group the Phase 4a draft into the canonical top-layer taxonomy without merging away scenario granularity” and listing “preserve scenario nodes…”, “grouping and refactor may not silently shrink coverage”, plus an “Anti-Compression Rule” prohibiting collapsing distinct scenarios.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: result.md is explicitly titled “Phase 4b” and argues alignment via Phase 4b-specific contract elements: “Phase 4b work: ‘spawn the canonical top-layer grouper’”, required artifacts “phase4b_spawn_manifest.json” and “drafts/qa_plan_phase4b_r<round>.md”, and a “Phase 4b post-validation gate” requiring coverage preservation and canonical layering.
